title: pypod run: ignore args intended for container command
body:
Don't try to argparse command-line arguments on the right-hand
side of the image; those are intended for the container command:

   pypodman run fedora find / -name foo
   pypodman run fedora bash -c 'echo hi'
   pypodman run fedora ls -l

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: Tested as best I can. I also considered importing `argparse.REMAINDER` into `podman_parser.py` and accessing it via redirection, but couldn't find a clean way to do so; perhaps there's something I missed.
@edsantiago I suspect there are other commands where I missed this nuance
`create` and `exec` come to mind; should I do a full sweep and resubmit?
@edsantiago If you have time, that would be great!  More eyes on the code never hurts. :+1:
OK, will do. Hold off on review/testing for now.

New force-push. Refactors `REMAINDER` so modules using it don't need to import argparse. Note that `pypodman exec` doesn't exist yet; if/when it does, it should also use this option for command. Note also that `pypodman attach` currently (misleadingly) has a `command` option; I'll look into it soon and submit a PR if one is called for.

For posterity, I checked for other podman commands via:
```
# for i in $(podman --help | head -52|tail -42|awk '{print $1}');do echo $i;man podman-$i | grep -w command;done | less
```
CI log is impossible to review. Are there any tools for making it human-readable?

@edsantiago Nope. These look properly unusual, though - usually it should print a summary of failures at the end, but it doesn't seem to have here

Is this rebased on top of master? I'm seeing test failures that I think we fixed with a commit yesterday.
It was branched from master as of 2 hours ago, but there are new commits and I've just rebased.
